title: El Nino likely strongest seen in 75 years, say US forecasters
summary: Experts say the phenomenon will strengthen through the end of the year.
imageAlt: El Nino likely strongest seen in 75 years, say US forecasters
aiSummary: Experts say the phenomenon will strengthen through the end of the year.
keyPoints: El Nino likely strongest seen in 75 years, say US forecasters
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de internacional.

multimedia.alt: El Nino likely strongest seen in 75 years, say US forecasters

fullArticle:
Sign up now: Get ST's newsletters delivered to your inbox

The phenomenon occurs when the sea surface temperature in the eastern and central equatorial Pacific is much warmer than normal.

WASHINGTON – The weather-roiling El Nino that emerged across the Pacific in June has continued to build and will likely be one of the strongest in more than 75 years, the US Climate Prediction Centre said.

Sea surface temperatures of 1 deg C or more, the hallmark of the phenomenon, have spread across the central and eastern equatorial Pacific, and there’s an 81 per cent chance it will become a very strong El Nino and rank among the “largest events in the historical record going back to 1950”, the agency said in its monthly forecast.
